corrected_fields.summary: This Federal Reserve Commercial Bank Examination Manual section (3000) provides supervisory guidance on assessing capital adequacy for state member banks. Effective November 2020, it outlines the purpose of bank capital, the framework of Regulation Q (12 CFR Part 217), and the three-tier capital structure (CET1, Additional Tier 1, Tier 2). The document does not address macro catalysts or market structure topics relevant to trading or investment decisions.
corrected_fields.why_it_matters: This is internal supervisory guidance for bank examiners, not a market-moving document. It defines how regulators assess bank capital adequacy but contains no economic forecasts, policy signals, or market-relevant catalysts.
